name: WordPress
description: WordPress — PHP CMS powering 40% of the web; supports headless mode via REST and GraphQL content APIs.

seo.title: WordPress — Backend-as-a-Service
seo.meta_description: WordPress: WordPress — PHP CMS powering 40% of the web; supports headless mode via REST and GraphQL content APIs.

when_to_use: Headless CMS via REST API and WPGraphQL for modern frontends
Publishing platform powering 40% of websites globally
Extensible CMS with 60K+ plugins for any site use case
Multi-site network for agencies managing many client sites

pros: Powers ~40% of the web; massive ecosystem
Headless mode via REST and WPGraphQL
Content editors already know the UX
cons: Performance and security depend on plugins
PHP monolith feels dated for API-first stacks
Scaling headless WP needs real caching work
review.verdict: Still the pragmatic content backend when editors matter, but expect plugin-driven pain if you push it headless.
review.best_for: Content teams needing a familiar CMS with optional headless REST/GraphQL output
review.not_for: Greenfield API-first products where a Node/Go headless CMS fits better
